score_db.comment: Phase 1 collect-only. Phase 2 will add percentile-based admission via panel_buy_pctile.

BULL_CALM._sdl_reason: 2026-05-04: replaced absolute 6% threshold with σ-scaled 3·daily_vol. B2 holdout showed absolute mode tripped on noise for high-vol stocks (NVDA/RBLX daily σ≈4-5%, threshold of 6% = ~1.3σ — common noise day). 20 SDL exits, 60% lost, median pnl −5.2%. With sdl_n_sigma=3, threshold = 3·(NGBoost_σ/√5). Per-ticker; low-vol stock σ=2% → threshold 6% (legacy); high-vol σ=5% → threshold 15% (more permissive on noise). Set max_single_day_loss_pct=0 so σ mode dominates; absolute mode comes back if NGBoost σ unavailable for a position.
BULL_CALM._bb14_meta_reason: 2026-05-11 P4.5 winner: BB_14 stops + meta-label classifier. Validated on 11-mo OOS 2025-04→2026-03: APY +0.2% / MaxDD 44.7% vs baseline -0.3% / 55.8%. Meta-label vetoed 5 SDLs. See doc/research/meta-labeling-results-2026-05-11.md.

CHOPPY._max_hold_days_reason: 2026-06-11 max_hold RFC (operator-approved, orchestrator #94): CHOPPY=40 was the lone per-current-regime tightening — shorter than the 60d thesis horizon, so it cut bets before evaluation (force-sold MU, a +momentum winner, at 45d after the regime fix flipped BEAR->CHOPPY; compounded by a missing entry-regime anchor falling back to the CURRENT regime). Tenure is not a regime property: unify at the far zombie-position backstop (500, matching all other regimes) so signal/risk exits (panel/rotation, model_protection, stops) own real exits per de Prado vertical-barrier reasoning. A uniform value also neutralizes the anchor-fallback hazard. Shadow uses the same exit backstop so readonly comparisons do not preserve the obsolete CHOPPY clock exit.

bear_defensive_sleeve._reason: 2026-06-07 Codex prep for native BEAR defensive sleeve task: explicit default-off switch so pipeline implementation can be deployed without changing production behavior before A/B validation.

sleeve._comment: 2026-07-02 S7 parking-sleeve config companion (renquant-pipeline #157 ParkingSleeveShadowTask). Lineage: RS-1 parking-sleeve decision memo r2 (renquant-orchestrator doc/research/2026-07-02-rs1-parking-sleeve.md) + 104 capability program §1.3 / P1-2 (doc/design/2026-07-02-104-capability-program.md). INERT: enabled=false keeps the task a byte-identical no-op; values here mirror the pipeline's safe defaults so defining the keys changes nothing. Flipping enabled=true starts SHADOW JSONL logging only (mode=live is reserved/unimplemented in #157 and falls back to shadow with a warning); real exposure additionally requires the RS-1 §4 pre-registered cash/SGOV/SPY replay comparison + a separately recorded capital authorization. beta_pos=1.0 is RS-1 §2's PROVISIONAL planning heuristic, not a measured risk budget. SGOV price coverage is an UMBRELLA follow-up (daily price fetch is umbrella-owned; shadow tolerates SGOV absence by logging qty=null per #157 deviation 3) — SGOV is deliberately NOT added to the watchlist so the alpha universe / cross-sectional admission stats never include a T-bill ETF. Mirrored into the readonly shadow config per the optional-section house pattern (bear_defensive_sleeve precedent).

intraday_decisioning._comment: 2026-07-03 renquant105 Stage-1 SHADOW arming — control-plane section for the intraday session scheduler (RFC #208 doc/design/2026-06-30-renquant105-intraday-decisioning-architecture.md §8.3/§10; consumer: renquant-orchestrator #266 intraday_session_scheduler.load_intraday_config; this is #266's landing-checklist step 3 config half). Values mirror the scheduler's safe defaults exactly: tick_seconds=720 (§5 fixed 12-min Stage-1 cadence), entry_open_delay_seconds=300 (no entries in the first 5 min after the open), entry_close_cutoff_seconds=1800 (no NEW entries in the last 30 min; exits continue to the bell), canary_allowlist=[] (no per-ticker restriction while shadow-only), kill_switch_file=null (scheduler default <data_root>/data/rq105/intraday_decisioning.KILL; the file is re-checked every cycle, so touching it halts mid-session). enabled=true is ONE of three independent gates and is INERT on its own: the scheduler is a SEPARATE process (launchd job, nothing in the daily run invokes it) and additionally requires the env kill switch RENQUANT_INTRADAY_DECISIONING to be truthy AND the kill-switch file to be absent — the machine-side install (plist bootstrap + env flag) is a separately authorized ask-first landing step. mode=shadow is the ONLY implemented mode: #266 runtime-asserts never-submit on every tick (assert_shadow_never_submits raises ShadowModeViolation before persisting) and structurally DOWNGRADES mode='live' to shadow with a counted warning (§9.3a); Stage-2 live authorization is a separate future decision and must arrive as a config change that fails tests/test_strategy_configs.py's shadow-only pin first. Mirrored into the readonly shadow config per the optional-section house pattern (sleeve / bear_defensive_sleeve precedent).

panel_exit._deployment_reason: 2026-05-11 audit fix: legacy PanelConvictionExitTask had 0/12336 historical fire rate due to calibrator-saturation rank_score gating. NEW CrossSectionalPanelExitTask post-PanelScoringJob uses raw panel_score with cross-sectional percentile (AND mu) + strong-mu OR-bypass. Catches BA-style cases: panel=+0.198 (32%ile) mu=-0.123 → fires via strong_mu bypass.

long_short._comment: Phase 2 long-short. Flip enabled=true to turn shorts ON; false to disable instantly. Pre-flight checklist before enabling in live: data/alpaca_borrow_status.json fresh (<7d), Alpaca paper account has margin/short permissions, max_shorts kept conservative (<=3 for first 2 weeks).

joint_actions._qp_cost_kappa_reason: 2026-05-23 Codex churn fix: QP turnover penalty must be at least round-trip fee+slippage = 2*(0.0005+0.0005)=0.002. The prior 0.0001 underpriced trading by 20x and let small-edge rebalances dominate.
joint_actions._qp_turnover_max_reason: 2026-05-23 Codex churn fix: explicit global daily L1 turnover cap. Regime-specific overrides may be tighter; BULL_CALM uses 0.15 so the system can still accumulate but cannot churn the book aggressively on marginal signals.
joint_actions._qp_min_dw_pct_reason: 2026-05-04: raised 0.005→0.02 to stop QP micro-trim (was killing strategy via 91 sub-$500 sells with full ST tax + slippage; sells avg +13% gross but net -3.6%).
joint_actions._qp_min_share_floor_pct_reason: 2026-05-24 Codex QP safety fix: disabled whole-share floor. Continuous Markowitz/Boyd QP weights are the constraint contract; integer execution may round down when target delta is below one share, but must not round up to one share and exceed solver target/caps. Re-enable only with fractional-share execution or a mixed-integer lot-size optimizer.

qp_admission_gate._min_expected_return_reason: 2026-05-24 Codex QP alpha contract: QP may size only candidates with finite calibrated expected-return evidence on the same 60d horizon as panel_ltr/QP μ. Requiring 4% 60d expected excess return in BULL_CALM keeps thin probability-only signals from becoming trades after friction and benchmark-sleeve opportunity cost.
qp_admission_gate._reason: 2026-05-23 Codex alpha-conversion fix: QP may size/rebalance only pre-qualified alpha. WF trace forensics showed negative raw-panel and low-rank QP/TopUp adds were net negative after tax/friction, and QP opened risk when the book was already at the legacy slot cap. Require calibrated rank >=0.55, raw panel >=0, and available slot for new names before emitting QP buys/top-ups.

joint_actions._qp_tax_aware_reason: 2026-05-04: explicitly DISABLED. tax_cost_vec made QP avoid selling big-gain positions, mechanizing the disposition effect — strategy crystallized small wins repeatedly while big winners reversed and erased gains. Violates the 'tax = reporting only, no decision logic' mandate (feedback_no_tax_driven_logic.md).

qp_soft_sell_guard._min_holding_days_reason: 2026-05-24 Codex exit-path audit: BULL_CALM QP soft sells had high false-positive continuation rates after exit while hard risk exits remain armed. QP trims are model-driven soft exits, so they should not cut a 60d panel thesis before its own horizon unless a path-risk rule fires.
qp_soft_sell_guard._apply_tax_gates_reason: 2026-05-23 Codex audit: QP soft sells still need the thesis-age horizon guard, but tax/LT suppression is disabled while qp_tax_aware=false so the order-emission stage cannot reintroduce hidden tax-driven hold logic.

joint_actions._qp_tax_lot_method_reason: G7 (2026-05-04): tax-lot disposal rule. 'fifo' = oldest lot first (broker default); 'hifo' = highest-cost lot first (minimises realized gain on partial sells); 'avg' = legacy single-cost-basis kill-switch.
joint_actions._qp_no_trade_band_cap_reason: 2026-05-09 BUG #7: σ-derived no-trade band uncapped → high-σ holdings (e.g. BA σ̂=0.24) had 24% band, structurally uncoverable. Cap at 5% so any single-position rebalance >5% always fires.
joint_actions._qp_min_invested_edge_reason: 2026-05-23 Codex alpha-conversion fix: disable forced cash deployment while QP/TopUp admission is being repaired. Cash drag previously let marginal candidates become trades; re-enable only after WF shows benchmark-relative alpha survives the strict admission gate.
joint_actions._qp_horizon_contract_reason: 2026-05-23 Codex audit: calibrator mu is a 60d forward-return estimate while realized-vol fallback is annualized; Markowitz QP requires mu and Sigma on the same single-period horizon, so sigma is scaled by sqrt(60/252) before covariance construction.
joint_actions._qp_ledoit_wolf_lambda_reason: 2026-05-10 C3: bumped 0.0→0.2 (Ledoit-Wolf 2004 mid-of-recommended-range). 169-stock universe sample-cov is high-noise / near-singular without shrinkage; λ=0.2 lifts off-diagonals toward 0 and equalises variance toward avg. Reference: Ledoit & Wolf 2004 Honey paper (J.PortMgmt 30(4):110-119).
joint_actions._qp_sector_cap_enabled_reason: 2026-05-10 C2: hard linear sector cap inside QP solve. Total sector weight ≤ max_positions_per_sector × max_position_pct. Closes audit gap where stress reallocation could exceed sector cap that buy-side filter enforced.
joint_actions._qp_correlation_cap_enabled_reason: 2026-05-10 C2: hard linear group cap on high-corr pairs (|ρ| ≥ correlation_guard_threshold). wp[i] + wp[j] ≤ 2 × max_position_pct. Linear approx of pair-product cap (Boyd 2004 §4.4).
joint_actions._qp_c2_infeasible_policy_reason: 2026-05-24 Codex audit: sector/correlation caps are hard diversification constraints. If they make the QP infeasible, block QP orders for the bar instead of relaxing or dropping risk constraints. Diagnostic configs may explicitly set relax/drop.
joint_actions._allow_cap_compliance_sells_on_infeasibl: 2026-06-06 Codex daily-shadow audit follow-up: keep qp_c2_infeasible_policy=strict, but allow the tested cap_compliance_fallback to emit risk-reduction sells that reduce existing over-cap holdings. This does not relax sector/correlation caps and never admits new buys.
joint_actions._qp_mu_contract_reason: 2026-05-21 Codex audit: QP objective expects μ in expected-return / volatility units. Strictly reject raw rank_score/panel_score fallback; valid sources are calibrator expected_return, NGBoost μ, or alpha_to_mu.
joint_actions._qp_band_method_note_2026-05-30: Operator opt-in to Davis-Norman closed-form no-trade band (research C). Defaults: dn_floor=0.005, dn_ceiling=qp_no_trade_band_cap (0.05), dn_eps_oneway=qp_cost_kappa/2 (0.001), dn_gamma=qp_risk_aversion (3.0). Replaces 5% ad-hoc band_cap (binding on most names) with literature 1/3-power band ~0.5-2% at typical σ. Per Davis-Norman 1990 / Janeček-Shreve 2004.

qp_live_shadow_telemetry._reason: 2026-06-08 Codex QP Step5 activation: renquant-pipeline live-shadow wiring is merged. Candidate matches fwd_1d current-QP replay evidence; this writes readonly JSONL telemetry only and must not change orders.

rotation._target_horizon_days_20260610: 2026-06-10 fix: stale 20 survived the 2026-06-05 GBDT demotion copy; prod/golden aligned to 60 earlier (umbrella 6d7a030). weekly_wf_promote derives its WF eval config from THIS pinned file; with 20 the calibrated ER is tagged horizon-20 vs qp_mu_horizon_days=60, so qp_admission_expected_return_horizon excluded every candidate (955/cut) -> zero trades across all WF cuts. Calibrator artifacts are lookahead_days=60. Empirical: same 2024Q1 cut with 60 trades +5.4%/3mo, 11 buys, est Sharpe +2.10.

model_params._lookahead_removed_2026-05-10: Per §5.13.5 single-source-of-truth: this key shadowed panel_ltr.lookahead_days and produced ambiguity (model_params.lookahead=5 vs panel_ltr.lookahead_days=10 vs prod artifact=60). Canonical key is now panel_ltr.lookahead_days. Tournament-side code (training/features.py build_training_features) still defaults to 5 internally — behavior unchanged for tournament path, but config no longer carries a conflicting value.

ranking._2026-05-19_shadow_purpose: Shadow config for full-e2e shadow run. Cloned from strategy_config.json, swapped panel scorer XGB→HF PatchTST. Hard-isolated state via --broker readonly-alpaca (broker_name=alpaca_shadow → live_state.alpaca_shadow.json + runs_alpaca_shadow.db). Run AFTER prod via daily_104.sh. ntfy gets [SHADOW] prefix.
ranking._2026-05-22_patchtst_contract: Updated from old canonical seed44 to strict-contract PT07 seed44: tail-val 10%, 60BD embargo, train-only label winsor, seq_len=24, lr=1e-4, wd=0.3, warmup=0.1. Final min-regime IC=+0.0307. This is a cleaner shadow instrument, not a promotion claim.

panel_scoring._signal_gate_prefer_calibrated_mu_reason: 2026-06-10 root-cause fix for structural sell-only. PatchTST is a cross-sectional ranker whose raw panel_score is intrinsically all-negative (output head centres near raw=-0.20; signal is in the relative ordering, OOS IC~0.13). The calibrator's ER=0 neutral sits at raw=-0.198, so calibrated mu>0 means 'ranked above the model neutral' = genuinely bullish. The legacy raw>0 direction gate assumes 0=neutral and blocks 100% of names (verified 0/142 admitted asof 2026-06-10), so the book can never open a long. With this flag the direction test becomes calibrated mu>0 alone (mu>0 <=> raw>neutral_raw), admitting the top-ranked names (80/142; e.g. DDOG/SMCI/FTNT/QCOM/CRWD mu +5-6pct, P 0.62-0.65). Requires pipeline signal_gate_prefer_calibrated_mu support (PR #98).
panel_scoring._buy_floor_reason: 2026-06-11 false-BEAR audit P2 (operator-approved audit; pipeline #113): adaptive_mean_std (max(0.20, mean+1*std), uncapped) is shape-unstable on the Platt-compressed PatchTST calibrator (rank_score IQR=0.039 on 2026-06-11; dropped 71/83=86%) and structurally caps breadth at ~16% regardless of edge (Grinold FLAM: IR=IC*sqrt(BR)). adaptive_quantile admits the top (1-q)=20% of the cross-section by calibrated rank — breadth becomes a deliberate parameter, stable under score compression. buy_floor_min=0.20 remains the absolute fail-safe on degenerate cross-sections. Requires pipeline #113.

sizing._note_20260609: conviction multiplier mapped raw panel_score with GBDT floor=0/ceiling=0.3; pt07 centers -0.19 -> multiplier 0 zeroed every buy. Kelly target already encodes conviction.

ngboost._disable_reason: 2026-05-09 EVENING REVERT after SIM-A vs SIM-B 27-month A/B (E55): NGB-on APY=+2.99% Sharpe=+0.26 trades=455 LOSES to NGB-off APY=+6.77% Sharpe=+0.40 trades=303 by -3.78 APY pts / -0.14 Sharpe. The σ-aware sizing degrades position selection AND increases churn vs. simple rank-only QP. Pure-alpha component too weak (~+0.018) to justify the friction it introduces. NGB head artifact retained for future use; flag stays off until either pure_alpha ≥+0.04 or alternative sizing scheme proves itself.

fingerprint._comment: 2026-07-03 M6 stage-2 step 1 (renquant-orchestrator doc/design/2026-07-03-m6-stage2-fingerprint-migration.md sec.3 step 1; reader merged as renquant-pipeline #164 fingerprint_dispatch.accept_legacy_stamps, absent => true). Explicit true == the reader's default, so merging this changes nothing running today: it declares the migration window (version-dispatched verification accepts legacy versionless stamps alongside schema-v1) in policy so the future flip is a reviewable config PR instead of a code-default change. Flipping to false is the deliberate stage-2 STEP-4 migration act (v1-only: a versionless stamp then fails closed with the re-stamp-under-v1 remedy), gated on the step-3 census green window -- rewrite the pin in tests/test_strategy_configs.py only alongside that recorded decision.

edge_sharpe_floor._disable_reason: 2026-05-09 (E52 audit): pure-alpha ceiling on current panel ~+0.029, no τ achieves target FDR=0.30 OR 0.40. Best τ=0.39 → FDR=0.42 admits 1% of val rows = 0 candidates per day in production. Gate would reject all signals; better to skip filter and let QP / sigma_sizing handle quality. Re-enable when val μ-IC ≥ +0.04 + placebo |IC|<0.01.

panel_scoring._2026-05-19_kind_fix: renamed from 'panel_ltr_xgboost' to 'xgb' to match model_registry. commit 9a6cca1 (2026-05-18) introduced registry-dispatch in LoadScorerTask. Synced from strategy_config.golden.json (also fixed). Without this fix LoadScorerTask logs ERROR + returns False, panel scorer never runs, candidates get only per-ticker scores, QP returns optimal_no_signal, zero trades.

sentiment._2026-05-18_provenance: Tier 2 SCREEN per regime-stratified IC eval; see doc/research/2026-05-18-news-sentiment-ic-verdict.md

shadow_models.name: xgb_alpha158_fund_previous_primary
shadow_models._2026_06_05_role: Previous primary scorer moved to strategy_config.shadow.json after PatchTST promotion.

regime_admission._promotion_reason_2026_06_05: Operator-directed PatchTST promotion from shadow to primary. Current HF checkpoint does not yet carry strict WF regime-admission metadata, so runtime regime admission remains disabled for this scorer; preflight/model contract, calibrator strict_scorer_match, QP, freshness, drawdown, and sell-only risk gates remain active.

panel_scoring._2026_06_05_patchtst_promotion: 2026-06-05 operator-directed prod/shadow switch: HF PatchTST pt07 strict seed44 promoted to primary scorer; XGB moved to readonly shadow config.

kelly_sizing._sigma_horizon_days_reason: 2026-06-03 Kelly audit shadow A/B: match the 60d calibrator mu horizon by scaling annualized realized-vol sigma to 60d before Kelly sizing; do not promote without OOS/placebo gates.
kelly_sizing._2026-05-15_activation_reason: Phase 3 of calibrator P0 (commits b16e2a1, 00f94ff, 342309e). Pre-fix: mu_none=33 across every panel, Kelly returned 0 for every candidate, QP fell back to uniform 10.17% sizing. Post-fix: c.mu = c.expected_return (bounded ±0.20 by train-site + load-site clips); c.sigma = trailing 60d annualized realized vol (clipped to [0.05, 1.50]). Calibrator artifact refitted with bounded ER (2026-05-15 15:52).

deep_drawdown_veto._disable_reason_2026-05-17: DISABLED 2026-05-17: Hou-Xue-Zhang 2020 (RFS) Replicating Anomalies showed 65% of published anomalies fail under NYSE-breakpoints + value-weighted returns. The distress / 52w-loser anomaly is among the failed ones in modern data. 5/17 e2e showed DDV vetoed META (rank #1, μ=+0.146, mega-cap $1.4T → distress effect weakest per FF 2008) on dd=-22.2%. Author's own docstring warned this costs +25pp in BULL_CALM/BULL_STRONG rallies. Config retained for potential regime-conditional re-enablement in BEAR/CHOPPY only.

buy_quality_gates._2026-05-15_activation_reason: Upgrades A+B from META trade evaluation. A: shrink rank_score by 0.5 when SPY regime is momentum-dominated (BULL_CALM/BULL_VOLATILE + hurst>0.65) but candidate's own 60d return is negative. B: veto candidates >20% below 52w high unless SUE or PEAD score is positive. Catches META-class trades (META today: r60=-4.66%, dd=-22.1%, no SUE → would have been score×0.5 then vetoed). See Asness-Moskowitz-Pedersen 2013, Hong-Stein 2003.

meta_label._deployment_reason: 2026-05-11: meta-label exit veto. López de Prado AFML ch.20. Trained 2026-05-11 on 2024-04→2025-04 (146 events, class balance 55.5%, AUC 0.554). Threshold 0.5 used in OOS validation; do NOT switch to artifact's F1-optimum 0.30 without re-validation. Fallback: §5.13.10 — missing artifact → no-op.

ranking._2026_06_05_shadow_switch: Readonly shadow now runs previous XGB primary scorer; production primary is HF PatchTST.
ranking._2026_06_23_shadow_switch: production primary is XGB (alpha158_fund); HF PatchTST runs here as readonly shadow.

panel_ltr._lookahead_days_reason_2026-05-10: Production artifact (panel-ltr.alpha158_fund.json trained 2026-05-09) has lookahead_days=60 / label_col=fwd_60d_excess. Prior config value (10) silently diverged from the artifact produced by scripts/train_production_model.py, which hardcodes 60. Setting config to 60 makes panel_ltr.lookahead_days the single source of truth (§5.13.5) and matches the production training horizon (CLAUDE.md project-status: 'XGBoost rank:pairwise + alpha158 + 5 fund + 3 PEAD + 3 SUE = 169 features, fwd_60d label').

ngboost.training_notes: Stage-2 NGBoost head for residual returns

hourly._disabled_reason: 2026-05-04 user mandate: daily model trains on daily-only data. Half the universe (~100/183 tickers) lacks intraday history; aggregating to daily creates NaN-leaf rows that polluted the calibrator (the 2026-05-04 incident). Re-enable only for an explicit hourly-resolution model (renquant_105).

minute._disabled_reason: Same as hourly above — daily model uses daily-only data by default.

challenger._doc: Phase 4 — shadow-mode infrastructure (platform only; live wiring in Phase 4b).

_activation_log.change: add LITE + COHR to watchlist (datacenter_hw / optical-comms exposure)
_activation_log.rollback: remove from watchlist + sector_map; revert this commit

_min_reentry_days_provenance: 2026-05-18 13:55 PT: MCD sold +$14.02 yesterday, model picked again today $5 higher = $15 round-trip loss. min_reentry_days=5 (business days) blocks all re-buys within 5d regardless of P/L sign — compounds with wash_sale_days (§1091, loss only). Default 5d = ~1 trading week, enough for sentiment + technical signals to materially change.

sizing._comment: 2026-07-09 P1 SHADOW: one-share floor ENABLED in shadow for comparison against production (OFF). Consumer: renquant-pipeline task_selection.py SizeAndEmitTask. Shadow runs on separate broker state (alpaca_shadow) so enabling here does NOT affect live orders. Mirrored into the readonly shadow config per the optional-section house pattern.

deployment_governor._comment: 2026-07-09 D5 PREPARE per orchestrator RFC #443 (Deployment Governor top-down sizing architecture). enabled=false in ALL THREE configs INCLUDING this shadow config (Codex review, strategy-104 PR #50): shadow=true with uncalibrated placeholders would be a silent future arming, not an inert prepare. S1 shadow arming will be a SEPARATE dedicated PR after (1) RFC #443 approval, (2) the renquant-pipeline deployment_governor reader (pipeline PR #179) is merged, and (3) D6 nested-selection tuning on the frozen tuning subset produces a frozen tuned config — that future arming PR flips ONLY this shadow flag and records the exact config + artifact fingerprints. Consumer: pipeline PR #179 reader (defaults enabled=false). All numeric values are PLACEHOLDERS pending D6 — they must NOT be treated as calibrated. Production enablement requires the D6 preregistered replay gate + S2 canary + recorded operator sign-off.

execution._settlement_reason_2026_05_24: US equities moved to T+1 settlement on 2024-05-28 under SEC Rule 15c6-1 amendments. Sim uses Alpaca-like non-marginable buying power so executed sell proceeds can fund buys without using 2x/4x margin buying power; set buying_power_mode=settled_cash for cash-account stress tests.

fractional_shares._comment: S-FRAC v2 stage-2 sizing contract (renquant-pipeline #153 / doc/design/2026-07-02-s-frac-fractional-v2.md / doc/design/2026-07-07-104-105-cash-drag-resolution.md): declare the fractional-sizing policy in strategy config instead of relying on pipeline code defaults, but keep it DEFAULT OFF until the active-path capability gate, broker guard, and sizing-fidelity evidence are all proven. While disabled, 104 stays on the safe whole-share + A-3 fallback path. Mirrored into the readonly shadow config per the optional-section house pattern (parking sleeve / intraday decisioning precedent).
fractional_shares._provenance: 2026-07-07 cash-drag phase-1 follow-up: strategy-104 owns the operator-visible enablement bit and the sizing-policy defaults; execution owns broker validation, pipeline owns sizing math, orchestrator owns scorecard monitoring.

software_stops._comment: 2026-07-09 S-FRAC v2 stage-3 PREPARE per D7 (orchestrator #444). Consumer = renquant-pipeline software_stops.SoftwareStopRegistry.from_config, which reads exactly execution.software_stops.{enabled, registry_path, max_staleness_minutes}; its is_armed() is the probe commit_contract.software_stops_armed calls. INERT while enabled=false: from_config returns None, the layer does not exist, stage-0 semantics untouched. registry_path and max_staleness_minutes mirror the reader defaults exactly (DEFAULT_REGISTRY_PATH / DEFAULT_MAX_STALENESS_MINUTES), so declaring them changes nothing. enabled=false in ALL THREE configs INCLUDING this shadow config: arming the protection layer is a live-safety act, not a shadow experiment. Arming = separate future PR gated on the stage-3 shadow packet + pager SLA demonstration + operator sign-off of the machine-death risk assumption.
